1_neoplasia.q: What is a tumor (neoplasm)?
1_neoplasia.l: A tumor is an abnormal clonal proliferation of cells.

1_neoplasia.q: Which feature best describes a benign tumor?
1_neoplasia.l: Benign tumors are localized, well differentiated and non-invasive.

1_neoplasia.q: Which feature best describes a malignant tumor?
1_neoplasia.l: Malignant tumors are invasive, poorly differentiated and capable of metastasis.

1_neoplasia.q: What is metastasis?
a: Spread of malignant cells to distant sites
1_neoplasia.l: Metastasis is the spread of malignant cells to distant sites.

1_neoplasia.q: What is hyperplasia?
1_neoplasia.l: Hyperplasia is an increase in the number of cells.

1_neoplasia.q: What is hypertrophy?
1_neoplasia.l: Hypertrophy is an increase in the size of cells.

1_neoplasia.q: What is neoplasia?
1_neoplasia.l: Neoplasia is autonomous, uncontrolled and disorganized growth of cells.

1_neoplasia.q: Goiter results from which combination?
1_neoplasia.l: Goiter is caused by hyperplasia and hypertrophy of thyroid follicular cells.

1_neoplasia.q: What are the two major components of a tumor?
1_neoplasia.l: Tumors consist of parenchyma and stroma.

1_neoplasia.q: Which component of a tumor determines its behavior and classification?
1_neoplasia.l: Parenchyma determines tumor behavior and classification.

1_neoplasia.q: What is the function of tumor stroma?
1_neoplasia.l: Stroma provides blood supply and structural support.

1_neoplasia.q: The suffix '-oma' usually indicates what type of tumor?
1_neoplasia.l: The suffix '-oma' usually indicates a benign tumor.

1_neoplasia.q: Which of the following is a malignant exception to the '-oma' rule?
1_neoplasia.l: Melanoma is malignant despite ending in '-oma'.

1_neoplasia.q: Which of the following is another malignant exception to the '-oma' rule?
1_neoplasia.l: Lymphoma is malignant despite ending in '-oma'.

1_neoplasia.q: What is an adenoma?
1_neoplasia.l: An adenoma is a benign epithelial tumor arising from glandular tissue.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a papilloma?
1_neoplasia.l: Papilloma is a benign epithelial tumor with finger-like projections.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a cystadenoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Cystadenoma is a benign cystic epithelial tumor.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a carcinoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Carcinoma is a malignant tumor of epithelial origin.

1_neoplasia.q: Which of the following is an example of carcinoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Squamous cell carcinoma is a malignant epithelial tumor.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a sarcoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Sarcoma is a malignant tumor of mesenchymal origin.

1_neoplasia.q: Which of the following is an example of a sarcoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Fibrosarcoma is a malignant tumor of mesenchymal origin.

1_neoplasia.q: Which of the following is another example of a sarcoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Liposarcoma is a malignant tumor arising from adipose tissue.

1_neoplasia.q: Osteosarcoma is a malignant tumor derived from which tissue?
1_neoplasia.l: Osteosarcoma is a malignant tumor of bone.

1_neoplasia.q: Chondrosarcoma is a malignant tumor derived from which tissue?
1_neoplasia.l: Chondrosarcoma is a malignant tumor of cartilage.

1_neoplasia.q: Which of the following is a mesenchymal tissue?
1_neoplasia.l: Bone is a mesenchymal tissue.

1_neoplasia.q: Which of the following is NOT a mesenchymal tissue?
1_neoplasia.l: Thyroid epithelium is epithelial, not mesenchymal.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a pleomorphic adenoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Pleomorphic adenoma is a benign salivary gland mixed tumor.

1_neoplasia.q: Pleomorphic adenoma contains which elements?
1_neoplasia.l: Pleomorphic adenoma contains epithelial and mesenchymal-like elements.

1_neoplasia.q: What is the malignant counterpart of pleomorphic adenoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Carcinoma ex pleomorphic adenoma is the malignant mixed tumor.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a teratoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Teratomas contain tissues derived from more than one germ layer.

1_neoplasia.q: What is another name for a mature cystic teratoma?
1_neoplasia.l: A mature cystic teratoma is also called a dermoid cyst.

1_neoplasia.q: Which feature best describes a mature cystic teratoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Mature cystic teratomas are benign and well differentiated.

1_neoplasia.q: Which type of teratoma is malignant?
1_neoplasia.l: Immature teratomas are malignant and contain immature tissues.

1_neoplasia.q: Having more than 50 melanocytic nevi increases the risk of which malignancy?
1_neoplasia.l: More than 50 melanocytic nevi increase the risk of melanoma.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a primary tumor?
a: Tumor originating in the organ where it is found
1_neoplasia.l: A primary tumor originates in the organ where it is found.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a secondary tumor?
1_neoplasia.l: A secondary tumor is a metastatic tumor from another site.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a hamartoma?
a: Disorganized mature tissue native to the organ
1_neoplasia.l: Hamartoma is normal tissue in an abnormal arrangement.

1_neoplasia.q: Which phrase best describes a hamartoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Hamartoma is normal tissue in an abnormal arrangement.

1_neoplasia.q: Pulmonary hamartoma classically appears as what on chest X-ray?
1_neoplasia.l: Pulmonary hamartoma commonly presents as a coin lesion.

1_neoplasia.q: Which tissues are commonly found in a pulmonary hamartoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Pulmonary hamartomas contain cartilage, fat and connective tissue.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a choristoma?
1_neoplasia.l: A choristoma is normal tissue in an abnormal (ectopic) location.

1_neoplasia.q: Which phrase best describes a choristoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Choristoma is normal tissue in an abnormal location.

1_neoplasia.q: Which is an example of a choristoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Pancreatic tissue in the stomach wall is a classic choristoma.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a Ghon focus?
a: Primary subpleural lung lesion of tuberculosis
1_neoplasia.l: A Ghon focus is the primary subpleural lesion of tuberculosis.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a Ghon complex?
a: Ghon focus plus hilar lymph node involvement
1_neoplasia.l: A Ghon complex consists of a Ghon focus and hilar lymph node involvement.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a granuloma?
1_neoplasia.l: A granuloma is a collection of activated macrophages (epithelioid histiocytes).

1_neoplasia.q: Which cytokine activates macrophages and promotes granuloma formation?
1_neoplasia.l: Interferon-γ activates macrophages and promotes granuloma formation.

1_neoplasia.q: What is multinodular goiter (MNG)?
1_neoplasia.l: Multinodular goiter is a hyperplastic enlargement of the thyroid gland.

1_neoplasia.q: Which benign lesion is a common differential diagnosis of a coin lesion in the lung?
1_neoplasia.l: Pulmonary hamartoma is a common cause of a coin lesion on chest X-ray.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a Peutz-Jeghers polyp?
1_neoplasia.l: Peutz-Jeghers syndrome is associated with hamartomatous polyps.

1_neoplasia.q: What is a hemangioma?
1_neoplasia.l: Hemangioma is a benign proliferation of blood vessels.

1_neoplasia.q: Trophoblastic tumors arise from which tissue?
1_neoplasia.l: Trophoblastic tumors arise from placental epithelium.

1_neoplasia.q: What is the benign counterpart of hepatocellular carcinoma?
1_neoplasia.l: The benign counterpart of hepatocellular carcinoma is hepatic adenoma.

1_neoplasia.q: A malignant epithelial tumor is called what?
1_neoplasia.l: Malignant epithelial tumors are called carcinomas.

1_neoplasia.q: A malignant mesenchymal tumor is called what?
1_neoplasia.l: Malignant mesenchymal tumors are called sarcomas.

1_neoplasia.q: The prefix 'Adeno-' refers to which structure?
1_neoplasia.l: Adeno refers to glandular tissue.

1_neoplasia.q: Papilloma is characterized by what microscopic appearance?
1_neoplasia.l: Papillomas have finger-like epithelial projections.

1_neoplasia.q: Which phrase best describes a hamartoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Hamartoma is normal tissue in an abnormal arrangement.

1_neoplasia.q: Which phrase best describes a choristoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Choristoma is normal tissue in an abnormal location.

1_neoplasia.q: What is the defining feature of a teratoma?
1_neoplasia.l: Teratomas contain tissues derived from multiple germ layers.

1_neoplasia.q: Goiter is best described as which combination?
1_neoplasia.l: Goiter results from both hyperplasia and hypertrophy.
